{"key_event":"Liberal Republic’s Revolution on the March reforms","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened as electoral turnover, legal union recognition, labor and peasant mobilization, and challenges to Church-landlord dominance expanded organized actors, though depression weakened export elites and mobilization remained uneven. State power rose modestly: depression initially cut revenues, but interventionist reforms, the Leticia War mobilization, public works, labor regulation, secular education policy, tax reform, and constitutional/property reforms broadened central authority and administrative reach.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":6.3,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":7.1}
